purpose: Practical planting-plan recipes used by the generator so zones read like designed plant communities; v101 makes density mean visible coverage, not hidden overlap.

poolPlanter.plantCountBehavior: moderate to full visible coverage, repeated rhythm, no confetti and no hidden overlap
poolPlanter.layoutDirective: Use clean repeated evergreen/foliage masses with grasses or strappy foliage as rhythm plants. Agave, yucca, and succulents are accents only. Avoid heavy flower drop, leaf litter, toxic/caution plants, and bee-heavy flowers at the pool edge.

slopePlanting.plantCountBehavior: fewer species repeated in visible drifts; high density fills more of the zone without hiding plants inside overlaps
slopePlanting.layoutDirective: Build a soil-holding matrix with sprawling shrubs/groundcovers, deep-rooted grasses/sedges, and low shrubs. Use seasonal flowers and succulents only as accents.

flowerBed.plantCountBehavior: layered masses/drifts; more plants than shrub zones, but still repeated groups
flowerBed.layoutDirective: Use low/front, middle/body, and tall/back flowering drifts. Repeat color groups and use limited foliage support.

grassDrift.plantCountBehavior: visible clumps and drifts; high density spreads clumps wider instead of stacking symbols on top of each other
grassDrift.layoutDirective: Use one dominant grass/sedge/lomandra species and one optional accent grass for color or height. Place as repeated clumps.

hedgeRow.plantCountBehavior: density means percent of the edge covered; plants are evenly spaced
hedgeRow.layoutDirective: Use one clippable evergreen hedge plant. Keep it visually rectangular/hedge-like, not round blobs.

rockGarden.plantCountBehavior: rocks are anchors; plants tuck around rocks in visible small groups, not hidden stacks
rockGarden.layoutDirective: Place rocks first as anchors, then tuck low mats, architectural accents, and color accents around them. Avoid a zone that is only succulents.
